structured_payload_candidate.title: Trim & Respond Logic General Operation
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Systems using Trim & Respond setpoint reset (e.g., VAV AHU static pressure)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Trim & Respond logic resets the setpoint within SPmin to SPmax. When device is OFF, setpoint = SP0. When active, every time step T, if R ≤ I, trim setpoint by SPtrim; if R > I, respond by changing setpoint by SPres*(R-I) but no more than SPres-max.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Associated device proven ON, after delay Td
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Every time step T: if requests ≤ ignored, trim setpoint by SPtrim; if requests > ignored, respond by SPres*(R-I) capped at SPres-max.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Trim & Respond logic shall reset the setpoint within the range SPmin to SPmax. When the associated device is OFF, the setpoint shall be SP0. The reset logic shall be active while the associated device is proven ON, starting Td after initial device start command. When active, every time step T, if R≤I, trim the setpoint by SPtrim. If there are more than I requests, respond by changing the setpoint by SPres*(R – I), (i.e., the number of requests minus the number of ignored requests) but no more than SPres-max.

evidence_quote: See Section 5.1.14.4  for an example of T&R implementation.
anchor_repair_reason: evidence_quote replaced with verbatim section heading line
evidence_text: See Section 5.1.14.4  for an example of T&R implementation.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Faulted Equipment Detection for Chillers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Chiller fault conditions include safety shutdown alarm, manual shut off (Local/Auto switch), status off 5 minutes after start command, CHW isolation valve not open 90 seconds after command, CW isolation valve not open 90 seconds after command, or low delta-T condition for 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger response.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Safety shutdown alarm; chiller manually shut off; chiller status remains off 5 minutes after command to start; CHW isolation valve not open 90 seconds after command; CW isolation valve not open 90 seconds after command; for 10 minutes, CHWR at least 3°C above CHWST setpoint and delta-T less than 2°C.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Safety shutdown alarm condition either through network or hardwired alarm contact; Chiller is manually shut off as indicated by the status of the Local/Auto switch from chiller gateway; Chiller status remains off 5 minutes after command to start; CHW isolation valve feedback indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open; CW isolation valve feedback indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open; For 10 minutes, chilled water return temperature has been at least 3°C (5°F) above the CHWST setpoint, and delta-T across the chiller has been less than 2°C (3°F).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Faulted Equipment Detection for Boilers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Boiler fault conditions include safety shutdown alarm, HW isolation valve not open 90 seconds after command, or leaving water temperature remains 8.3°C below setpoint for 15 minutes with delta-T less than 6°C.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger response.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Safety shutdown alarm; HW isolation valve not open 90 seconds after command; boiler leaving water temperature remains 8.3°C (15°F) below setpoint for 15 minutes and delta-T less than 6°C (10°F).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Safety shutdown alarm condition either through network or hardwired alarm contact; HW isolation valve feedback indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open; If boiler leaving water temperature remains 8.3°C (15°F) below setpoint for 15 minutes and delta-T across the boiler has been less than 6°C (10°F).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Faulted Equipment Detection for Cooling Towers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Cooling tower fault conditions include tower fan failure, inlet isolation valve not open 90 seconds after command, or outlet isolation valve not open 90 seconds after command.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 2 alarm and trigger response.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Tower fan has failed; inlet end switch indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open; outlet end switch indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Tower fan has failed as defined above; Inlet end switch indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open; Outlet end switch indicates valve is not open 90 seconds after valve is commanded open.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Response to Faulted Fan, Pump, or Cooling Tower
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon fault, command on the next off equipment (B) while alarming equipment (A) remains commanded on. If B fails, repeat until required quantity proven on or no more equipment. Set alarming equipment to last position in staging order. Non-alarming equipment follow even wear logic.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fault detected on operating equipment A
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Command on next off equipment B; if B fails, repeat; move A to last position; non-alarming equipment follow even wear logic.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Upon identification of a fault condition: i. For fans, pumps, and cooling towers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment 'B', shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment 'A' remains commanded on. (b) If Equipment 'B' fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), it shall remain commanded on and the preceding step shall be repeated until the quantity of equipment called for by the current stage has proven on, or there are no more available equipment. (c) Set alarming equipment to the last positions in the lead/lag or lead/standby staging order sequenced reverse chronologically (i.e., the equipment that alarmed most recently is sent to last position). (d) Staging order of non-alarming equipment shall follow the even wear logic.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Response to Faulted Chiller or Boiler
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon fault, command on next off equipment (B) while alarming equipment (A) is commanded off and set to last position. If B fails, repeat until required quantity proven on or all equipment tried. If all tried and insufficient non-alarming equipment, most recently alarmed equipment remains commanded on.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fault detected on operating equipment A
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Command on next off equipment B; command off A and set to last position; if B fails, repeat; if all tried and insufficient, most recently alarmed remains on.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Upon identification of a fault condition: ii. For chillers and boilers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment 'B', shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment 'A' is commanded off and set to the last position in the lead/lag staging order. (b) If Equipment B fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), repeat the preceding step until the quantity of equipment called for by the lead/lag logic have proven on or until all equipment has been tried. (c) If all equipment has been tried and the quantity of non-alarming equipment is less than called for then the most recently alarmed equipment will remain commanded on.
